question_text: Explain why ice floats on water, even though they are made of the same substance.
correct_answer: Ice floats because it is less dense than water. When water freezes, it expands and becomes less dense, so it floats on the denser liquid water.
explanation: This is a real-world example of how density affects whether something floats or sinks. Ice has a lower density than liquid water because the water molecules are arranged more loosely when frozen, taking up more space.
